title: BA (Hons. with Research) Film Production and Theatre
content: The BA (Hons. with Research) in Film Production and Theatre is a 4-year undergraduate programme me designed for students passionate about cinema and performing arts. Aligned with NEP 2020, it offers a balanced blend of creative practice and academic inquiry, with special focus on direction, acting, cinematography, screenwriting, and stagecraft. Students receive hands-on training through film shoots, theatre productions, workshops, and internships, along with interdisciplinary courses, skill enhancement modules, and value-added learning. In the final year, those securing a CGPA of 7.5 or above may opt for a research project or dissertation, guided by faculty, to explore specialized topics within film and theatre. Graduates are equipped for diverse roles in the film industry, theatre, television, OTT platforms, and higher studies or research, with a strong foundation in both artistic and scholarly approaches to storytelling. Students with a CUET / SAT (DI Code - 71057 ) score will be given preference in the merit list For Migration from Other Universities: 1) B.A (Hons/Hons with Research) Film Production and Theatre Ist yr. minimum 50% marks or equivalent 2) XII with minimum 50% marks in aggregate 01 To cultivate creative professionals with strong storytelling, performance, and technical skills across film and theatre production. 02 To provide hands-on training in direction, acting, screenwriting, cinematography, and stagecraft through real-world projects and industry exposure. 03 To foster critical thinking and research capabilities by integrating theoretical frameworks with a final-year research project or dissertation. 04 To prepare students for diverse careers in cinema, theatre, OTT platforms, and academia by combining artistic practice with strategic, interdisciplinary learning. 01 Technical know-how: In-depth, hands-on across the key disciplines of filmmaking and theatre: Directing, Screenwriting, Cinematography, Sound, Editing, Production (including Production Design) and 1st Assistant Directing. 02 Writing Script: Practical training for script writing for niche audience across the genre for films and theatre production. 03 Training: Practicing film & Theatre work with industry experts teaching up-to date skills using industry standard equipment and software. 04 Art of Film-making: Developing filmmaking practice while acquiring skills for professional work in the creative industries of film and theatre. 05 Modern tool usage: Hands – on training over the software and tools used in industry. 06 Project management and finance: Synthesize knowledge and understanding of the films and communication principles and apply these to one’s own work, as a member and leader in a team, to manage core art projects and in multidisciplinary environments. 07 Environment and sustainability: Elaborate the impact of the professional art performer and communication solutions for societal and environmental contexts; demonstrate the creative knowledge for promotion of sustainable development. 08 Ethics: Integrate ethical principles and commitment to professional ethics, responsibilities and norms of the art practice. 09 Team work: Engage with innovative, ambitious ideas, developing successful films and plays in groups and individually, and gain a practical knowledge of a range of filmmaking and theatrical approaches. 10 11 01 Engage in professional entrepreneurial activities to work as acknowledged leaders in Film Production. 02 Work as a creative artist in the theatre/film industry. 03 Undertake higher education in Film studies and research organization. 01 Design short films and content for the OTT Platforms 02 Perform as artists for a short film. Students with a CUET / SAT (DI Code - 71057 ) score will be given preference in the merit list 1) B.A (Hons/Hons with Research) Film Production and Theatre Ist yr. minimum 50% marks or equivalent 2) XII with minimum 50% marks in aggregate
